segments.text: And he began to speak unto them by parables.
And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
And when they were come, they say unto him,
Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar, or not?
segments.speaker: Pharisees and Herodians

segments.text: Shall we give, or shall we not give?
segments.speaker: Pharisees and Herodians

segments.text: But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them,
And they brought it. And he saith unto them,
Cæsar's
segments.speaker: Pharisees and Herodians

segments.text: And Jesus answering said unto them,
And they marvelled at him.
Then come unto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection; and they asked him, saying,
Master, Moses wrote unto us, If a man's brother die, and leave his wife behind him, and leave no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
segments.speaker: Sadducees

segments.text: Now there were seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and dying left no seed.
And the second took her, and died, neither left he any seed: and the third likewise.
And the seven had her, and left no seed: last of all the woman died also.
In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seven had her to wife.
segments.speaker: Sadducees

segments.text: And Jesus answering said unto them,
And one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him,
Which is the first commandment of all?
segments.speaker: Scribe

segments.text: And Jesus answered him,
Well, Master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God; and there is none other but he:
segments.speaker: Scribe

segments.text: And to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbour as himself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
segments.speaker: Scribe

segments.text: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him,
And no man after that durst ask him any question.
And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple,
And he said unto them in his doctrine,
And Jesus sat over against the treasury, and beheld how the people cast money into the treasury: and many that were rich cast in much.
And there came a certain poor widow, and she threw in two mites, which make a farthing.
And he called unto him his disciples, and saith unto them,
